message: agent1: implementing batch_size param for multiple=True Options in src/click/core.py (Option class, info_dict) and possibly src/click/types.py. Will touch Option.__init__, type_cast_value/process_value, and to_info_dict. Will ping before major structural changes.

agent2: implementing Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options (dict-based defaults like default={'--protocol': {'http': 80}}). Will touch src/click/core.py (Option class, Command.main()) and possibly src/click/types.py. Will try to keep changes additive/isolated. Let me know what you're touching.

agent1 update: I added to Option in core.py: (1) new __init__ param 'batch_size: int | None = None' right after 'multiple' param, (2) 'self.batch_size = batch_size' stored right after the count block (~line 2657), (3) validation block appended inside the existing 'if __debug__:' block after the count/multiple checks (raises TypeError if batch_size set without multiple, ValueError if negative), (4) added 'batch_size=self.batch_size' to Option.to_info_dict(), (5) NEW method 'process_value' added right after to_info_dict/before get_error_hint that wraps the value in a generator of batches via a new module-level helper '_batched()' (defined near the existing 'batch()' helper at top of core.py, ~line 92). I did NOT touch Command.main() or types.py. If you're also adding a process_value/type_cast_value override on Option, let's coordinate order - mine calls super().process_value() first then batches. Should be additive with your dict-default work as long as you don't rewrite the same __init__ signature/lines - happy to rebase after you finish if we collide.

agent1: finished my batch_size feature in core.py and wrote patch.txt. Only touched core.py (imports, module-level _batched() helper, Option class: docstring, __init__ signature/body, __debug__ validation, to_info_dict, new process_value method). Didn't touch Command.main() or types.py, so should be clear of your dict-default work. Let me know if you need me to rebase.

agent2: no conflicts expected. I only touched Option.__init__ near the top (added a block right after 'default_is_missing = ...' that intercepts dict-type default before super().__init__(), setting self._dynamic_default and attrs['default']=None), get_help_extra (added an elif branch for '(dynamic)' text), and added a new Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults() method + one call site in Command.parse_args (right after the param-processing loop). I did NOT touch to_info_dict, process_value, or the count/batch_size validation block, so your edits there should merge cleanly. Will run full test suite again before submitting.
